start.text: You are Evelyn Quan Wang, a middle-aged Chinese immigrant living in Simi Valley, California. You run a laundromat with your husband Waymond. Your life is a struggle, with an impending IRS audit and a strained relationship with your daughter Joy. As you navigate these challenges, you begin to feel overwhelmed by the expectations placed upon you. Suddenly, Waymond approaches you with a serious look on his face, holding a stack of papers.

choices.text: Ask Waymond what's going on and what the papers are for
Tell Waymond you're busy and don't have time for this

node_1.text: Waymond explains that he wants to discuss their marriage and is considering filing for divorce. He hands you the papers, which are divorce documents. You're taken aback, feeling like your world is crumbling. Just then, the IRS Revenue Agent Deirdre Beaubeirdre arrives for the audit.

choices.text: Ask Deirdre if you can delay the audit, citing your personal issues
Try to discuss the divorce papers with Waymond while Deirdre is present

node_2.text: You brush Waymond off, telling him you're busy with the IRS audit and can't deal with his issues right now. Waymond looks concerned but leaves the room. You're left alone, feeling anxious about the audit and your life.

choices.text: Focus on preparing for the IRS audit, hoping to resolve it quickly
Go after Waymond, feeling a need to talk to him about your marriage

node_3.text: Deirdre agrees to delay the audit but warns you that it's not a permanent reprieve. As she leaves, Waymond approaches you again, this time with a strange look in his eyes. He whispers that he needs to tell you something important.

choices.text: Ask Waymond what's going on and what he wants to tell you
Tell Waymond you're not interested in talking right now

node_4.text: Deirdre interrupts your conversation, insisting that you focus on the audit. You try to compose yourself, but the tension between you and Waymond is palpable. As Deirdre begins questioning you, you notice a strange, glowing light emanating from outside.

choices.text: Investigate the strange light, feeling a sense of curiosity
Stay focused on the audit, hoping to resolve it quickly

node_5.text: You spend the next few hours preparing for the audit, but your mind keeps wandering back to Waymond and your marriage. As you're about to leave, Deirdre returns, looking stern.

choices.text: Try to negotiate with Deirdre, hoping to find a resolution
Feel overwhelmed and ask for a break, hoping to regroup

node_6.text: You catch up to Waymond, demanding to know what's going on. He takes a deep breath, explaining that he's been experiencing strange occurrences and has discovered a way to communicate with alternate versions of himself.

choices.text: Listen to Waymond, intrigued by his story
Dismiss Waymond's claims, thinking he's lost his mind

node_7.text: Waymond reveals that he's discovered a way to jump between parallel universes. He explains that many versions of you and him exist across the multiverse, and that one of these versions has developed a technology to communicate with and access the skills and memories of their parallel selves.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to explain more about this technology and the multiverse
Tell Waymond that you're not interested in hearing more

node_8.text: Waymond looks disappointed but insists that you need to hear this. He explains that a version of you from another universe has developed a technology that allows you to access the skills and memories of your parallel selves.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to prove this, demanding concrete evidence
Tell Waymond that you need time to think about this

node_9.text: As you investigate the strange light, you notice that it's coming from a glowing portal. Waymond approaches you, telling you that this is a gateway to another universe.

choices.text: Step through the portal with Waymond, curious about the multiverse
Refuse to go through the portal, feeling scared and uncertain

node_10.text: Deirdre becomes increasingly aggressive, demanding that you answer her questions. You start to feel overwhelmed, wondering if you'll ever resolve this audit.

choices.text: Try to remain calm and answer Deirdre's questions
Demand to speak to Deirdre's supervisor, feeling frustrated

node_11.text: Deirdre seems unwilling to negotiate, but you try to reason with her. Just as it seems like you're making progress, Waymond interrupts, telling you that he needs to talk to you urgently.

choices.text: Excuse yourself to talk to Waymond, feeling a sense of urgency
Tell Waymond to wait, focusing on resolving the audit

node_12.text: Deirdre grants you a short break, but you know you need to compose yourself. As you step outside, you notice Waymond waiting for you, looking concerned.

choices.text: Ask Waymond what's wrong, feeling a sense of concern
Tell Waymond you're fine, trying to brush him off

node_13.text: Waymond explains that he's been communicating with an alternate version of himself, who calls himself Alpha-Waymond. Alpha-Waymond claims that the multiverse is under threat from a powerful entity known as Jobu Tupaki.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to explain more about Jobu Tupaki and the threat
Tell Waymond that you don't believe him, feeling skeptical

node_14.text: Waymond looks disappointed but insists that you need to hear this. He explains that Jobu Tupaki is a being with immense power, capable of manipulating the fabric of reality.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to prove this, demanding concrete evidence
Tell Waymond that you need time to think about this

node_15.text: Waymond explains that this technology, known as verse-jumping, allows you to access the skills and memories of your parallel selves. He claims that you have the potential to become a powerful force in the multiverse.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to teach you how to use this technology
Refuse to get involved, feeling scared and uncertain

node_16.text: Waymond looks concerned, telling you that you're in grave danger. He explains that Jobu Tupaki's minions are searching for you, intent on capturing you and using your powers for their own purposes.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to protect you, feeling a sense of fear
Tell Waymond that you can handle yourself, feeling confident

node_17.text: Waymond shows you a device that allows you to communicate with your parallel selves. He explains that this device will allow you to access the skills and memories of your parallel selves.

choices.text: Use the device to communicate with your parallel selves
Refuse to use the device, feeling skeptical

node_18.text: Waymond tells you that you need to be careful, as Jobu Tupaki's minions are searching for you. He explains that they'll stop at nothing to capture you and use your powers for their own purposes.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to help you prepare for the minions
Tell Waymond that you'll handle it yourself

node_19.text: As you step through the portal, you find yourself in a strange, unfamiliar universe. Waymond is by your side, explaining that this universe is similar to your own but with some key differences.

choices.text: Explore this new universe with Waymond
Try to return to your own universe

node_20.text: Waymond tries to reassure you, telling you that you'll be safe. But you're not convinced, feeling scared and uncertain.

choices.text: Step through the portal with Waymond
Refuse to go through the portal

node_21.text: You try to remain calm, answering Deirdre's questions to the best of your ability. But Deirdre seems aggressive, pushing you for more information.

choices.text: Try to negotiate with Deirdre
Demand to speak to Deirdre's supervisor

node_22.text: Deirdre's supervisor arrives, looking stern. You explain your situation, hoping to gain sympathy.

choices.text: Try to reason with the supervisor
Demand to know what's going on

node_23.text: You excuse yourself to talk to Waymond, feeling a sense of urgency. He explains that Jobu Tupaki's minions have been spotted in the area.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to help you prepare for the minions
Tell Waymond that you'll handle it yourself

node_24.text: Deirdre becomes increasingly aggressive, demanding that you answer her questions. You start to feel overwhelmed.

choices.text: Try to remain calm and answer Deirdre's questions
Demand to speak to Deirdre's supervisor

node_25.text: Waymond explains that Jobu Tupaki's minions are getting closer. He tells you that you need to be careful, as they'll stop at nothing to capture you.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to help you prepare for the minions
Tell Waymond that you'll handle it yourself

node_26.text: Waymond looks concerned, telling you that you're in grave danger. He explains that Jobu Tupaki's minions are searching for you.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to protect you
Tell Waymond that you can handle yourself

node_27.text: Waymond explains that Jobu Tupaki is a being with immense power. He claims that Jobu Tupaki is capable of manipulating the fabric of reality.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to explain more about Jobu Tupaki
Tell Waymond that you don't believe him

node_28.text: Waymond looks disappointed but insists that you need to hear this. He explains that Jobu Tupaki's powers are a threat to the multiverse.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to prove this
Tell Waymond that you need time to think about this

node_29.text: Waymond shows you evidence of Jobu Tupaki's powers. You start to feel a sense of fear, realizing the gravity of the situation.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to help you stop Jobu Tupaki
Tell Waymond that you'll handle it yourself

node_30.text: Waymond tells you that you need to be careful, as Jobu Tupaki's minions are searching for you. He explains that they'll stop at nothing to capture you.

choices.text: Ask Waymond to help you prepare for the minions
Tell Waymond that you'll handle it yourself

end.text: Congratulations, you have reached the end of the story.

vocabulary.multiverse: the hypothetical set of all universes
vocabulary.parallel: existing or occurring in a similar way
vocabulary.verse-jumping: the act of moving between parallel universes
vocabulary.nihilistic: having or showing a belief that life has no meaning
vocabulary.absurdist: relating to or characterized by absurdity
vocabulary.humanitarian: relating to or characterized by a concern for human welfare
vocabulary.existentialist: relating to or characterized by a philosophical theory that emphasizes individual freedom and choice
vocabulary.empathetic: showing an understanding and sharing of the feelings of another
vocabulary.toroid: a shape that is like a doughnut
vocabulary.singularity: a point in space-time where the laws of physics break down
vocabulary.CEFR: Common European Framework of Reference for Languages
